channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rise Mzansi calls for tighter regulation amid surge in gambling licences
item.description: South Africa’s gambling sector is issuing more than 3,000 new licences each year, sparking growing unease about oversight and enforcement. Parliamentary data shows 3,135 licences were granted in the 2...

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau Triumphs at Steyn City with R119 Million Payday
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau claimed a massive R119 million at the inaugural LIV Golf South Africa tournament held at The Club at Steyn City. The American star took home R68 million for winning the individual ti...

item.title: Chappell Roan Responds to Jorginho’s Accusation of Security Guard’s Behavior Toward His Family
item.description: American pop artist Chappell Roan responded publicly after Brazilian soccer star Jorginho accused one of her security guards of acting aggressively toward his wife, Catherine Harding, and his 11‑year‑...

item.title: Makinde, Bala Mohammed, and Seriake Dickson Meet Amid PDP Crisis
item.description: Governors Seyi Makinde of Oyo and Bala Mohammed of Bauchi, alongside Senator Seriake Dickson of Bayelsa, are meeting in Bauchi amid rising tensions in the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). Sources sugge...

item.title: Govt imposes higher levy on high-octane fuel to support economy
item.description: In a move to address the economic strain caused by rising fuel prices, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has directed an increase in the levy on high-octane fuel by Rs200 per litre. This adjustment, appli...

item.title: Ruto shifts key government function from CS Ruku’s ministry to new office
item.description: In this article, the Daily Nation explains why President William Ruto moved a key government function from the docket of Cabinet Secretary Geoffrey Ruku, who is in charge of Public Service, Human Capi...

item.title: Delve Faces Allegations of Misleading Customers on Compliance Practices
item.description: Delve, a Y Combinator-backed compliance automation startup, has been accused of misleading hundreds of customers into believing they were fully compliant with privacy and security regulations, potenti...

item.title: BJP slams ex-Pakistan envoy for remarks on attacking India, calls Pakistan a terrorist state
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has strongly condemned the remarks made by former Pakistani diplomat Abdul Basit, calling Pakistan a 'terrorist state' and accusing it of relying on terrorism for surv...

item.title: Alberta poultry producers prepare for spring bird migration amid ongoing avian influenza threat
item.description: Poultry farmers across Alberta are taking extra precautions as spring migration brings large numbers of waterfowl back to the region, raising concerns about a potential resurgence of avian influenza. ...

item.title: Social media influencer killed in suspected murder-suicide in Islamabad
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Islamabad on the first day of Eidul Fitr, where a social media influencer named Sana Javed, known online as OutLofara, was allegedly shot dead by her husband, Muhammad Sa...

item.title: Stormers secure bonus-point win against Dragons to rise to second on URC log
item.description: The Stormers managed a hard-fought 29-21 win over the Dragons at DHL Stadium on Sunday, securing a crucial bonus point and moving up to second place on the United Rugby Championship (URC) log. Sacha F...

item.title: Samantha Sotto Yambao reflects on grief and purpose in her new novel 'The Elsewhere Express'
item.description: Filipina author Samantha Sotto Yambao, fresh from her success with 'Water Moon', explores themes of grief, purpose, and self-discovery in her new novel 'The Elsewhere Express'. The story began while s...

item.title: UST men reach UAAP tennis finals after ousting NU
item.description: In an exciting UAAP tennis match, the University of Santo Tomas (UST) men's tennis team triumphed over the National University (NU) to secure their spot in the finals for the upcoming season. The UST ...

item.title: No Winners in PCSO March 22 Lotto Draws; 6/49 Jackpot Rises to ₱131M
item.description: No bettors hit the jackpot in the major lotto draws of the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) on Sunday night, March 22, 2026. The Super Lotto 6/49 jackpot has now reached ₱131,201,705.31 af...

item.title: Trump and Iran exchange escalating threats over infrastructure as Middle East conflict intensifies
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East has seen tensions rise dramatically as U.S. President Donald Trump and Iran exchange aggressive threats targeting critical infrastructure. Iran has vowed to retaliat...

item.title: Shift in Nyanza politics as leaders embrace Ruto administration while Orengo faces backlash
item.description: Leaders from Nyanza region are increasingly engaging with President William Ruto’s administration, signalling a noticeable shift in the political landscape of the Luo‑Nyanza belt. Once seen as a stron...

item.title: Police investigate disappearance of former CS Raphael Tuju and his driver in Karen
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju and his driver, Steve Mwanga, have been reported missing after their vehicle was found abandoned in the Karen area of Nairobi on Saturday evening. According to Tu...

item.title: Health PS Mary Muthoni Engages Murang’a Residents in Jamii Imara Mashinani Forum on Local Development Issues
item.description: Health Principal Secretary Mary Muthoni spearheaded the Jamii Imara Mashinani (JIM) citizen engagement forum in Gatanga Constituency, Murang’a County, on March 21, 2026. The event, held at Gatanga CCM...

item.title: Reviving Democracy: Local Government Accountability and the Fight for Human Rights
item.description: In commemorating the Sharpeville Massacre, Lesego Mahlangu calls for a renewed focus on local government accountability to honour the memory of those who died for dignity and equality. Despite the 199...

item.title: Police Officer Dies in Collision While Responding to Emergency Call
item.description: A police officer has tragically passed away after a serious crash while responding to an emergency call. PC Bradley Corke, 27, died on Saturday from critical injuries sustained in a collision on Thurs...
